title: India Releases National Family Health Survey-6
summary: The NFHS-6 (2023-24) reveals significant progress in maternal and child health. Institutional deliveries reached 90.6%, and antenatal care coverage rose to 95.9%. Full immunization coverage improved from 83.8% to 87.1%, while child nutrition indicators showed positive trends, with stunting reduced by 17% and severe wasting by 32%. The survey covered 6.79 lakh households across 715 Indian districts.

title: National Health Accounts Estimates 2022-23 Released
summary: India's Government Health Expenditure (GHE) grew threefold from ₹1.30 lakh crores in 2013-14 to ₹3.85 lakh crores in 2022-23. GHE as a share of GDP rose from 1.15% to 1.43%. Notably, out-of-pocket expenditure declined from 64.2% to 43.4% of total health spending, while government spending on primary healthcare more than doubled to ₹1.4 lakh crores over the decade.

title: India Intensifies Ebola Preparedness Measures
summary: Union Health Minister Shri J P Nadda reviewed Ebola preparedness following WHO's declaration of a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. While India reports zero cases of Bundibugyo Ebola, the government has intensified screening at international airports. The ICMR and NCDC are on high alert for tracking and testing, with updated SOPs shared across all States and Union Territories.

title: India Advises Travel Caution Amid Ebola Outbreak
summary: The WHO declared the Bundibugyo strain Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern on 17 May 2026. With high mortality and no approved vaccines, India advises citizens to avoid non-essential travel to DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan. Authorities are strengthening surveillance at entry points to detect febrile illnesses from these high-risk affected areas.

title: ABDM Achieves 100 Crore Linked Health Records
summary: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission has reached a milestone of 100 crore health records linked to ABHA accounts. This represents a doubling of records from 50 crore in February 2025 within just 15 months. Over 450 public and private health tech solutions are now integrated, facilitating a secure, paperless, and interoperable digital healthcare ecosystem across various Indian states.

title: India Showcases TB Screening Efforts at WHA
summary: At the 79th World Health Assembly, Union Health Minister J P Nadda highlighted India's 'TB-Mukt Bharat' vision. The initiative utilizes AI-assisted tools, digital chest X-rays, and the 'Khushi' multilingual chatbot to enhance early detection. India is deploying decentralized testing systems to reduce diagnostic delays in underserved regions, aiming to build a resilient, people-centered health system for lung screening.

title: ICMR and ICAR Launch SEHAT Mission
summary: The Indian Council of Medical Research and Indian Council of Agricultural Research launched 'SEHAT', a national mission-mode programme. This initiative focuses on agriculture-nutrition-health convergence to shift India from curative to preventive healthcare. The programme prioritises biofortified crops, integrated farming systems, and NCD prevention, aiming to translate agricultural advancements into measurable public health outcomes through a whole-of-government and whole-of-system approach.

title: NHA Concludes AB PM-JAY AI Hackathon
summary: The National Health Authority, IndiaAI Mission, and IISc Bengaluru concluded the AB PM-JAY Auto-Adjudication Hackathon Showcase 2026. Over 3,500 participants competed to develop AI-driven solutions for health claims adjudication to enhance transparency and speed. Winning teams received cash awards up to ₹5 lakh, with the NHA focusing on scaling these innovations to prevent fraud and improve programme integrity.

title: Union Health Ministry Launches Integrated Training for Primary Healthcare Teams
summary: Union Minister Jagat Prakash Nadda launched a competency-driven Integrated Training framework for primary healthcare teams. This initiative shifts from fragmented capacity building to a structured model, empowering the frontline workforce. Notably, it supports women who comprise over 70% of the workforce, including ASHAs, ANMs, and CHOs, to ensure high-quality, people-centered care and last-mile delivery for Viksit Bharat @2047.

title: Launch of JANANI Digital Platform for Maternal and Child Healthcare
summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare introduced JANANI, a digital platform for longitudinal health records of women of reproductive age. Upgrading the RCH portal, it has already achieved 1.34 crore beneficiary registrations, 30 lakh pregnant women registrations, and 30 lakh MCH cards. The system uses QR-enabled cards to track antenatal, postnatal, and newborn care, ensuring seamless continuity of care.
